About: It is about Armenian folk singing.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Eurovision Song Contest 2009.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Mane Akopyan.
HasA: It has a repertoire that includes duets and arias.
It has a second album titled Tamzara.
It has a third studio album titled Heartbeat of my Land.
It has a fourth album titled Sketches.
It has a song titled "Jan Jan".
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by folk singing.
HeldIn: It was held in Moscow, Russia.
It was held in Omsk.
It was held in Los Angeles.
It was held in Tehran.
It was held in New York City.
It was held in Toronto.
It was held in Argentina.
It was held in Paris.
It was held in London.
It was held in Germany.
IsA: It is an Armenian folk singing duo.
It is a group of sisters.
KnownAs: It is known as the Arshakyan Sisters.
KnownFor: It is known for representing Armenia in the Eurovision Song Contest 2009.
It is known for its folk music.
It is known for its albums We and our Mountains, Tamzara, Heartbeat of my Land, and Sketches.
MemberOf: It is a member of Genealogy.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
OccurredOn: It occurred on 24 December 1980.
It occurred on 1994.
It occurred on 2001.
It occurred on 1999.
It occurred on 2005.
It occurred on 18 March 1982.
It occurred on 1997.
It occurred on 1998.
It occurred on 2002.
It occurred on 2004.
PartOf: It is part of Armenia State Philharmonic Orchestra and Chorus.
It is part of the G. Achemyan violin ensemble.
It is part of the State Song Theater of Armenia.
It is part of SHARM Holding LLC.
ProducedBy: It was produced by A. Grigoryan.
PublishedAs: It was published as the album We and our Mountains.
It was published as the album Tamzara.
It was published as the album Heartbeat of my Land.
It was published as the album Sketches.
It was published as the single "Tamzara".
It was published as the single "Khlpane".
It was published as the single "Marry song".
It was published as the single "Jan Jan".
It was published as the single "Gutan".
It was published as the single "The road".
UsedFor: It is used for promoting activities.
It is used for representing Armenia in the Eurovision Song Contest.
It is used for promoting their album Heartbeat of my Land.
UsedIn: It is used in the Eurovision Song Contest 2009.
It is used in the Eurovision Song Contest 2015.
UsedTo: It is used to promote themselves as a writer/composer.
It is used to promote their activities.
It is used to represent Armenian folk music in a new way.
It is used to achieve victory in Moscow.
WrittenBy: It was written by Avet Barseghyan.
It was written by Vardan Zadoyan.
